metadata.title:
WATERPOWER C0MPANYAT


WORK ON HOWARD STREET

pages.raw_text:
Although the city council at the
meeting last Monday night passed
a resolution to stop the Washington
Water Power company from laying
tracks over the Howard street
bridges until 1t could consider the
offer of the Spokane Traction COM
pany Of $4,000 for the right of lay
ing one track beside that of the
Washington Water Power company,
the power company started work
yesterday on Howard street and
states it will push the line to COM
pletion.


The water power company with
drew its offer to give the city $4,000
for it's share of the rebuilding of
the new bridge over the north chan
mel of the river and defied the city
to stop them from continuing the
work. The bridge was to be rebuilt,
as the city engineer reported it to
